creator: koobs.freebsd@gmail.com
text:
Volodymyr,

Just a followup on my commit, since you expressed interest in additional
review:

- I removed DIST_SUBDIR, its mostly only useful where one port has
  many uniquely named distfiles. Also its unclear if the user would
  expect this in postgresql/ or mysql/ or be suprised if they found it
  there. So it's safer in this case to not "do" anything.

- portlint had a few warnings (one of which was FATAL), install ports-
  mgmt/porttools and/or ports-mgmt/portlint and run your port through
  it before submission, it is highly valuable and makes a committers
  job much easier.

- Try hard not to let vim write its meta data into your submissions,
  find a way to disable it.

- I added PYDISTUTILS_AUTOPLIST support to the port, removing the
  need to use pkg-plist in the future.

- I modified DISTNAME to match the PyPI name, this ensures that the
distfile is named correctly, as would be expected by users.

Also, there's no need to apologise. You're interested in improving your
skills, you're contributing to the community, and that is to be commended.

We're available on IRC if you need anything else, it's a much better
place to learn and collaborate than gnats/PR's :]

Thanks again for your submission
